questionText:
Transform the following direct statement into reported speech:
John said, "I live in Douala."
options: John said that he lives in Douala.
John said that he lived in Douala.
John said that I live in Douala.
John said that he is living in Douala.
explanation: On applique le 'tense backshift' : le présent simple ('live') devient passé simple ('lived') au discours indirect. 'I' devient 'he' pour correspondre au sujet 'John'. 'That' est optionnel mais courant.

questionText:
Rewrite in reported speech:
Mary said, "I am reading an interesting book."
options: Mary said that she is reading an interesting book.
Mary said that she was reading an interesting book.
Mary said that I was reading an interesting book.
Mary said that she reads an interesting book.
explanation: Le présent continu ('am reading') devient passé continu ('was reading') au discours indirect. Le pronom 'I' se change en 'she' pour référer à Mary.

questionText:
Change to reported speech:
They said, "We have visited the Limbe Wildlife Centre."
options: They said that they have visited the Limbe Wildlife Centre.
They said that we had visited the Limbe Wildlife Centre.
They said that they had visited the Limbe Wildlife Centre.
They said that they visited the Limbe Wildlife Centre.
explanation: Le present perfect ('have visited') recule au past perfect ('had visited') au discours indirect. Le pronom 'we' devient 'they'.

questionText:
Report the following sentence:
Peter said, "I will help you tomorrow."
options: Peter said that he would help me the next day.
Peter said that he will help me tomorrow.
Peter said that I would help you the next day.
Peter said that he would help you the next day.
explanation: 'Will' devient 'would'. L'adverbe de temps 'tomorrow' devient 'the next day' (ou 'the following day'). Le pronom 'you' se transforme en 'me' selon le contexte (Peter s'adresse à moi).

questionText:
Convert into reported speech:
She said, "I didn't go to the party yesterday."
options: She said that she didn't go to the party yesterday.
She said that she hadn't gone to the party the day before.
She said that she hadn't went to the party the day before.
She said that she didn't go to the party the day before.
explanation: Le past simple négatif ('didn't go') recule au past perfect ('hadn't gone') en discours indirect. 'Yesterday' devient 'the day before'.

questionText:
Change to reported speech:
"I can speak French and English," said the student.
options: The student said that he can speak French and English.
The student said that he could speak French and English.
The student said that I could speak French and English.
The student said that he was able to speak French and English.
explanation: Le modal 'can' au présent devient 'could' au passé en discours indirect (backshift des modaux possibles). 'The student' est remplacé par 'he'.

questionText:
Report this statement:
Mama said, "The baby sleeps well every night."
options: Mama said that the baby slept well every night.
Mama said that the baby sleeps well every night.
Mama said that the baby is sleeping well every night.
Mama said that the baby has slept well every night.
explanation: Même s'il s'agit d'une vérité générale ou d'une habitude, on applique généralement le backshift dans un contexte de discours rapporté standard ('sleeps' → 'slept'). En contexte camerounais, on suit la règle formelle.

questionText:
Rewrite in indirect speech:
He said, "I must finish my homework now."
options: He said that he must finish his homework now.
He said that he had to finish his homework then.
He said that he must finish his homework then.
He said that he musted finish his homework then.
explanation: En discours indirect, 'must' exprimant une obligation peut rester 'must' (soutenu) ou devenir 'had to' (plus courant). 'Now' devient 'then'. 'Had to' est la forme standard ici.

questionText:
Report the following:
"I love this song," she said.
options: She said that she loves that song.
She said that she loved this song.
She said that she loved that song.
She said that I love that song.
explanation: Le présent simple 'love' devient 'loved' (backshift). Le démonstratif 'this' (proche) devient 'that' (éloigné dans le temps/l'espace).

questionText:
Transform into reported speech:
The teacher said, "Paul has been studying hard for the exam."
options: The teacher said that Paul has been studying hard for the exam.
The teacher said that Paul had been studying hard for the exam.
The teacher said that Paul was studying hard for the exam.
The teacher said that Paul studied hard for the exam.
explanation: Le present perfect continuous ('has been studying') recule au past perfect continuous ('had been studying') en discours indirect. C'est un backshift complet.
